subject_options: Petals' 312 reviews deserve a stronger website
Helping Petals convert more local searches into bookings
One thing holding Petals back in Westport search results
email_body:
I work with local salons in Ireland on SEO and website improvements, and Petals came up while I was looking at well-reviewed businesses in the Westport area.

With 312 reviews at 4.8 stars, you have built one of the stronger local reputations in Mayo. What I noticed is that the website's structure makes it harder than it should be for new visitors to find your contact form or reach the booking flow — and with no content section in place, the site is not yet ranking for the local search queries that would bring those visitors in the first place.

I offer a Local SEO starter package that covers on-page optimisation across your key service pages, local schema implementation, and a simple content plan tailored to Westport and the surrounding area.

Would you be open to a short call this week so I can walk you through a few specific things I noticed and show you what a focused set of improvements could look like for Petals?

Best regards
[Your name]
cta: Would you be open to a short call this week so I can walk you through a few specific things I noticed and show you what a focused set of improvements could look like for Petals?
follow_up_body: I wanted to follow up on my email from last week about Petals' online visibility in Westport. I appreciate things get busy in a salon environment so I will keep this brief. The core offer is a Local SEO starter package covering on-page optimisation, schema, and a content plan to help the site bring in more new visitors alongside your strong word-of-mouth reputation. Is this something you would consider in the coming months, or is there someone else at Petals I should be speaking with about the website?
personalisation_tokens_used: 312 Google reviews at 4.8 stars — used to establish genuine local credibility and frame the gap between reputation and website performance
Contact form difficult to find — used as a specific, observable friction point in the enquiry pathway without shaming the overall site
